reasoning_chain: The unpaved gravel road with no center line and rural fencing is typical of remote agricultural areas in New Zealand’s South Island.
The presence of a center-pivot irrigation system on the right indicates large-scale farming, common in New Zealand’s Canterbury or Otago regions.
Vegetation consists of lush green pasture and scattered native trees, consistent with New Zealand’s temperate maritime climate.
